scope: SKELETON — proxy variable analysis only; no protected-class labels available in Home Credit dataset
hard_disclaimer: This is a fairness SKELETON, not a fairness CERTIFICATION. Home Credit dataset does not contain legally protected-class labels (race, sex, national origin). Proxy analysis on age, income, employment, and region is for governance awareness only. Do NOT claim this model is fair or disparate-impact compliant.

age_bands.notes: Age is not a protected class in credit lending under ECOA; included as governance proxy only

employment_proxy.notes: HC dataset: DAYS_EMPLOYED=365243 → not employed; EMPLOYED_YEARS>=50 treated as not-employed sentinel

governance_observations: Age <30 cohort: approval rate 80.5% vs 95.5% for age 50+ — age correlates with repayment history depth, not a protected-class proxy in this jurisdiction
Not-employed proxy (EMPLOYED_YEARS sentinel): lower DR (5.2%) than employed (8.7%) — model is not penalizing non-employment through this feature
Income terciles: narrow approval rate spread (89.0% low vs 91.3% high) — model is not strongly income-stratifying approvals
Region 3 (high-risk): 81.7% approval vs 96.5% for Region 1 — regional disparity aligned with observed default rate difference (11.2% vs 4.9%)
All observed differences are directionally aligned with actual default rate differentials — no evidence of model amplifying disparities beyond base rates
limitation_statements: No protected-class labels — disparate impact ratio cannot be computed
No fairness-aware retraining performed in this pass
Income is a feature — its direct use may proxy for protected characteristics in some jurisdictions
Monotone constraints prevent extreme extrapolation but do not eliminate proxy risk
Full fairness audit requires demographic enrichment, legal review, and independent validator
forbidden_claims: Do not claim: model is fair, disparate impact compliant, ECOA compliant, or ready for fair-lending review without independent audit
